summary: Broad mechanical formatting pass produced by running `make format`, touching 100 files across fighter, stage, Dolphin GX, baselib, item/library/player code, and headers. The PR was closed unmerged shortly after creation and has no body, comments, reviews, or explicit rationale beyond the title, so evidence supports classifying it as formatting-only cleanup rather than semantic decomp progress.
what_changed: Ran the repository formatter over a large set of C and header files, causing 1796 added and 1447 deleted lines across 100 changed files.

Rewrapped long initializer lists, especially fighter MotionState tables in character init files such as ftCl_Init.c, ftCa_Init.c, ftDk_Init.c, ftKp_Init.c, ftPr_Init.c, ftSk_Init.c, ftGk_Init.c, ftPe_Init.c, and many others.
Reformatted jump-table initializers in Dolphin GX files such as GXAttr.c, GXPerf.c, and GXTexture.c, converting dense one-line or long wrapped lists into formatter-controlled multi-line layouts.
Reordered at least one include block according to formatter/style rules, shown in ftCaptain/ftCa_Init.c where ftCa_SpecialHi.h appears before ftCa_SpecialLw.h.
Updated Cargo.lock package versions for local tooling crates: melee-replace-symbols from 0.1.0 to 0.2.0 and melee-utils from 0.1.0 to 0.1.1.
No PR body, review comments, or human discussion were present in the provided context; the PR was closed without merge.
smart_moves: Keeping the PR scoped to formatter output made the resulting diff mechanically inspectable, even though it was large.
The pass normalized highly repetitive data-heavy constructs such as MotionState tables and jtbl_t arrays, which can reduce future manual style churn if adopted consistently.
The diff preserved symbol names and table contents in the excerpt; changes shown are layout-oriented rather than renaming or control-flow edits.
decomp_lessons: Large formatter-only PRs can create substantial diff noise in a decompilation project; future work should prefer running `make format` before opening feature/matching PRs or isolate formatting commits from semantic commits.

Formatter output for long function-pointer tables should be treated as style churn unless accompanied by evidence of content changes; reviewers should verify table entries are not reordered or altered accidentally.
Data-heavy fighter init files and GX jump tables are especially sensitive to formatting churn because minor line wrapping can dominate the diff without changing matching behavior.
When changing formatting tooling, update lockfiles intentionally and document the formatter/tool version change so future diffs can be reproduced.
naming_conventions: Fighter character init files follow the ftXX_Init.c naming pattern, for example ftCl_Init.c, ftCa_Init.c, ftMh_Init.c, ftGw_Init.c, and ftDk_Init.c.
Motion state callback names in the excerpt use the pattern ft<character>_<action>_{Anim,IASA,Phys,Coll}, for example ftLk_SpecialNCharge_Anim and ftLk_SpecialNCharge_Coll.
Common fighter callbacks use the ftCo_ prefix, shown with entries such as ftCo_Zair_Anim, ftCo_BatSwing_Anim, and ftCo_ParasolSwing_Phys.
Dolphin GX jump-table labels in the excerpt use address-like names such as jtbl_804010F4, jtbl_80401528, and jtbl_804012B8, with target symbols like GXTexture_8033E7B4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff contains MWERKS_GEKKO guarded jtbl_t declarations in GXAttr.c and asm function context in GXTexture.c, but the provided evidence shows formatting of existing tables rather than a new matching tactic.
Formatter line wrapping was applied to static jump-table initializer arrays without changing their apparent order in the excerpt, a useful caution for preserving switch/jump-table matching data.
No decomp.me scratch improvement, codegen trick, or matching claim is documented for this PR; any matching impact is unsupported by the provided evidence.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR context.
Because the PR was closed unmerged and lacks discussion, there is no evidence of reviewer intent or accepted project policy from this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that merged formatter output or changed `make format` policy after pr-784.
Check whether the Cargo.lock tool version bumps in pr-784 were merged through a different PR.
Search for review guidance on separating formatting-only changes from decomp matching changes.
Compare later formatting of MotionState tables and jtbl_t arrays to see whether this formatter style became canonical.
